remember: Students will be able to recall the names and characteristics of the three orders (Nobility, Clergy and Commons) in Medieval Europe.
Students will be able to identify the privileges and duties of each order.
Students will be able to describe the hierarchical structure of the three orders.
understand: Students will be able to explain the role of the three orders in shaping the social and economic structure of Medieval Europe.
Students will be able to analyze the relationship between the three orders and the feudal system.
Students will be able to describe the impact of the Black Death on the three orders.
apply: Students will be able to apply the characteristics of the three orders to a specific historical scenario.
Students will be able to analyze a historical event or document in the context of the three orders.
Students will be able to evaluate the significance of the three orders in understanding Medieval European society.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the strengths and weaknesses of the three orders.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the social and economic structures of different regions in Medieval Europe using the framework of the three orders.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of the three orders in shaping the course of Medieval European history.

ncert_objectives: Explain the emergence of the three orders in Medieval Europe.
Describe the privileges and duties of each order and their relationship with the feudal system.
Analyze the impact of the Black Death on the three orders.
board_exam_objectives: Describe the characteristics of the three orders (Nobility, Clergy and Commons) in Medieval Europe.
Explain the role of the three orders in shaping the social and economic structure of Medieval Europe.
Analyze the impact of the Black Death on the three orders and the feudal system.
